name: backlog-coordination
title: Backlog Coordination
purpose: Multi-agent backlog and WIP coordination via per-change Temporal workflows + cross-entity search attributes. Eliminates drift between agent view and user view in single-developer-multi-agent workflows. GitHub Project remains canonical for ranking and Value (V); the change workflow itself acts as the durable claim record (no separate claim primitive, no project-level shared workflow).

requirements.title: Cross-session claim visibility via AdvBacklogIssueNumber search attribute
requirements.body: When a change workflow is created with origin.kind === 'roadmap' and origin.issue_number set, the workflow's AdvBacklogIssueNumber search attribute is populated from state.origin.issue_number. Peer agent sessions can detect existing claims by querying Temporal Visibility filtered on AdvAffectedProjects + AdvBacklogIssueNumber + non-terminal AdvChangeStatus. The change workflow itself IS the durable claim: status draft|pending|active = claim held; archived|closed = claim released. This mirrors the in-repo precedent set by AdvWorktreeBranches / AdvWorktreePaths (see plugin/src/temporal/search-attributes.ts).

scenarios.title: Search attribute populated when origin.issue_number set
given: A change workflow created with seedState.origin = { kind: 'roadmap', issue_number: 42 }
scenarios.when: buildChangeSearchAttributes runs on the workflow state
then: The result includes AdvBacklogIssueNumber: ['42'] (string value, single-element array per Temporal Keyword convention)

scenarios.title: Search attribute omitted when origin.issue_number absent
given: A change workflow with state.origin undefined OR state.origin.issue_number undefined
then: The result does NOT include an AdvBacklogIssueNumber key

scenarios.title: Peer session sees claim via Visibility query
given: Session A creates a change with origin.issue_number = 51 in project pid-abc
Search attribute propagation has completed (≤ ~5s on SQLite dev server)
scenarios.when: Session B queries Temporal Visibility with 'AdvAffectedProjects = "pid-abc" AND AdvBacklogIssueNumber = "51" AND AdvChangeStatus IN ("draft", "pending", "active")'
then: The response includes session A's change workflow ID

requirements.title: Atomic claim check at change-create time
requirements.body: adv_change_create with origin.kind === 'roadmap' and origin.issue_number set performs a Temporal Visibility query before workflow start. If any existing change workflow in the same project has the same AdvBacklogIssueNumber and non-terminal AdvChangeStatus, the tool returns a typed CLAIM_CONFLICT error including the existing change ID. The agent surfaces the conflict to the user (resume existing change, override, or cancel). The change workflow itself becomes the durable claim record on successful create.

scenarios.title: CLAIM_CONFLICT returned when active change exists for same issue
given: A change workflow exists in status 'active' with origin.issue_number = 51 in project pid-abc
scenarios.when: adv_change_create is called with origin_kind: 'roadmap', origin_issue_number: 51 in pid-abc
then: The tool returns an error response with code 'CLAIM_CONFLICT'
The response includes the existing change ID and its non-terminal status
No new change workflow is started

scenarios.title: Claim succeeds when no existing change holds the issue
given: No active change workflow exists with origin.issue_number = 99 in project pid-abc
scenarios.when: adv_change_create is called with origin_kind: 'roadmap', origin_issue_number: 99
then: The change workflow is created normally
The new change holds the claim (status draft, AdvBacklogIssueNumber = '99')

scenarios.title: Claim check skipped when origin.kind !== 'roadmap'
given: An existing active change holds origin.issue_number = 51
scenarios.when: adv_change_create is called with origin_kind: 'discovery' (no issue_number)
then: No Visibility query is fired
The new change is created independently

requirements.title: Post-create double-check window for race tolerance
requirements.body: Temporal Visibility is eventually consistent (up to 10s on SQLite dev servers per service.ts:94-101 documentation). Two simultaneous adv_change_create calls may both pass the pre-create check. After workflow start, adv_change_create re-runs the same Visibility query within a 5-second window (configurable). If more than one workflow shares the same AdvBacklogIssueNumber in the project, the tool returns a CLAIM_RACE_DETECTED advisory on the success response (the new change is NOT rolled back; the agent surfaces both change IDs to the user for resolution).

scenarios.title: Race detected when concurrent creates land within window
given: Two adv_change_create calls fire concurrently with origin.issue_number = 7 in the same project
Both pre-create Visibility queries return empty
scenarios.when: Each call completes its 5s post-create double-check
then: Each response includes a CLAIM_RACE_DETECTED warning with both change IDs
Both changes remain alive (caller decides resolution)

scenarios.title: No race warning on solo create
given: A single adv_change_create call with origin.issue_number = 8
No other concurrent call for the same issue
then: The Visibility query returns exactly one workflow (the new one)
The response does NOT include CLAIM_RACE_DETECTED

requirements.title: Single-call WIP visibility aggregator
requirements.body: adv_wip_state returns a single aggregated view of an ADV project's in-flight coordination state: (a) active changes (status IN draft|pending|active) from Temporal Visibility, (b) worktree state from the worktree state DB, (c) peer sessions from the session registry. One tool call per agent session; no client-side composition required. Read-only — does not mutate state.

scenarios.title: Aggregated response includes all three sources
given: Project pid-abc has 2 active changes, 1 active worktree, 1 peer session
then: The response includes active_changes: [...] with 2 entries
The response includes worktrees: [...] with 1 entry
The response includes peer_sessions: [...] with 1 entry
The response includes generated_at as ISO 8601 timestamp

scenarios.title: Partial failure surfaces as warning, not error
given: Session registry lookup fails (e.g., SQLite locked)
Active changes and worktrees succeed
then: The response includes active_changes and worktrees populated
The response includes peer_sessions: [] with a warning field describing the failure
The response is not classified as an error

requirements.title: WIP visibility exposes poisoned workflow metadata without hiding healthy state
requirements.body: adv_wip_state MUST preserve healthy active-change, worktree, and peer-session results when one worktree-owning change workflow cannot be queried. Per-workflow poison or query failures from the worktree source MUST be surfaced as human-readable warnings and as structured poisoned_workflows metadata optimized for automation and agent triage. The metadata MUST include source, changeId, workflowId, recoveryReason, evidenceSummary, and message when poison evidence is available. The tool remains read-only and MUST NOT perform destructive recovery.

scenarios.title: Poisoned worktree workflow does not hide healthy WIP
given: One change workflow returns healthy worktree records
A second change workflow query fails with poisoned-history evidence
then: The healthy worktree remains in worktrees
The response includes a warnings entry for the worktree source
The response includes poisoned_workflows with source="worktrees", changeId, workflowId, recoveryReason, evidenceSummary, and message

scenarios.title: Poison metadata is read-only triage data
given: poisoned_workflows contains a workflow with TMPRL1100, NonDeterministic, Nondeterminism, WorkflowTaskFailedCauseNonDeterministicError, No command scheduled, or WorkflowExecutionUpdateAccepted evidence
then: No terminate, reset, reseed, archive, or delete action is performed
Operators can use the metadata to choose a separate audited recovery path

requirements.title: Active-change cross-reference uses O(1) Visibility query
requirements.body: When the agent renders a backlog with active-change annotations (e.g., adv_backlog_state), it MUST use queryActiveChangesByIssueNumbers — a single Temporal Visibility query with an IN clause covering all snapshot issue numbers — and MUST NOT call store.changes.get() per change. This replaces the legacy O(n*m) buildActiveChangeIndex pattern in plugin/src/tools/roadmap.ts:703-737. Inputs > 100 issue numbers are batched at 100 per call to stay under Temporal query-string length limits.

scenarios.title: Single Visibility call for snapshots ≤ 100 issues
given: A snapshot with 50 features and 10 bugs (60 issue numbers total)
then: queryActiveChangesByIssueNumbers is called exactly once
store.changes.get() is NOT called from the annotation path

scenarios.title: Batched Visibility calls for snapshots > 100 issues
given: A snapshot with 250 issue numbers
then: The helper makes exactly 3 Visibility calls (250 / 100 = ceil 3)
Results are merged into a single Map<number, ClaimVisibilityResult>

requirements.title: Agent never writes Value (V) autonomously
requirements.body: The ranking model preserves human authority over Value: agent code paths that mutate backlog item metadata (claim, release, score-adjust, etc.) MUST treat the V field as read-only. Mutations may update TC, RROE, E based on agent analysis, but V flows only from human action (GitHub Project UI or explicit /adv-triage user-mediated assignment). WSJF formula (V * TC * RROE / E) is unchanged.

scenarios.title: Schema rejects agent attempts to write V
given: Any agent-facing tool that mutates backlog item metadata
then: The V field is absent from the mutation argument schema OR explicitly typed as read-only

scenarios.title: WSJF formula references registered GH Project field IDs
given: The .adv/github-project.json config defines fields { value, time_criticality, rroe, effort, wsjf }
then: The formula V * TC * RROE / E is preserved
No field is renamed or removed by this change

requirements.title: Snapshot annotation freshness TTL contract
requirements.body: Cached projection .adv/roadmap-snapshot.json carries optional TTL metadata: last_refreshed (ISO 8601), ttl_ms (default 300000 = 5 min), and next_refresh_after (computed). Consumers MUST treat absence of last_refreshed as 'stale, refresh now'. Default annotation TTL is 5 minutes — distinct from FILE_SNAPSHOT_STALE_AFTER_MS (2h) which covers WSJF-content staleness. The shorter window keeps active-change annotations fresh across multiple agent sessions while staying well under the 5000 req/hr gh CLI rate limit budget.

scenarios.title: Snapshot inside TTL window is fresh
given: A snapshot with last_refreshed = now - 2 minutes and ttl_ms = 300000
then: needs_refresh is false
age_ms is approximately 120000

scenarios.title: Snapshot past TTL is stale
given: A snapshot with last_refreshed = now - 6 minutes and ttl_ms = 300000
then: needs_refresh is true

scenarios.title: Snapshot without last_refreshed is treated as stale (backward-compat)
given: A legacy snapshot with no last_refreshed field
then: needs_refresh is true
age_ms is null

scenarios.title: Default ttl_ms applies when missing
given: A snapshot with last_refreshed set but ttl_ms absent
then: The default 300000ms (DEFAULT_ANNOTATION_TTL_MS) is used

requirements.title: Change Origin Linkage Matrix and Seed State
requirements.body: adv_change_create MUST validate origin linkage metadata before workflow start and seed valid origin metadata into the authoritative change workflow state. origin_issue_number is accepted only when origin_kind is 'roadmap' or 'triage'; 'roadmap' requires origin_issue_number. origin_source_artifact is accepted only when origin_kind is 'triage' or 'discovery' and MUST be non-blank when provided. 'roadmap' rejects origin_source_artifact. 'discovery' rejects origin_issue_number. 'adhoc' and omitted origin_kind reject both origin_issue_number and origin_source_artifact. Valid origin metadata MUST appear in workflow reads and must drive relevant search attributes, including AdvBacklogIssueNumber when an issue number is present. Implementations MUST NOT rely on a late disk-only origin patch after workflow start.

scenarios.title: Roadmap origin requires issue and rejects source artifact
given: adv_change_create is called with origin_kind: 'roadmap'
then: The invocation fails if origin_issue_number is absent
The invocation fails if origin_source_artifact is provided
A valid roadmap create seeds state.origin.issue_number before workflow start

scenarios.title: Triage origin accepts optional issue and source artifact
given: adv_change_create is called with origin_kind: 'triage'
scenarios.when: origin_issue_number or non-blank origin_source_artifact is provided
then: The linkage fields are accepted
The accepted linkage values are present in workflow state reads

scenarios.title: Discovery origin rejects issue and accepts non-blank source artifact
given: adv_change_create is called with origin_kind: 'discovery'
then: The invocation fails if origin_issue_number is provided
A non-blank origin_source_artifact is accepted and appears in workflow state reads

scenarios.title: Adhoc and omitted origin reject linkage fields
given: adv_change_create is called with origin_kind: 'adhoc' or with origin_kind omitted
then: The invocation fails before workflow start
The response names the disallowed linkage field

scenarios.title: Seeded issue metadata drives search attributes
given: A change workflow is created with valid origin.issue_number
then: AdvBacklogIssueNumber is populated from workflow state.origin.issue_number
The value is not sourced from a late disk-only patch

requirements.title: Triage source cleanup before issue creation and scoring
requirements.body: /adv-triage MUST run source cleanup validation after structural match/gap analysis and before new issue creation, bug Priority prompts, or feature Value prompts. Cleanup validation covers represented and unrepresented ADV changes, GitHub issues/project items, agenda items, and local sources gathered by /adv-triage. Each non-relevant cleanup candidate MUST include source, stable ref, classification, evidence, proposed action, and approval group. Heuristics such as title similarity or agent inference are advisory only and MUST NOT close, complete, cancel, remove, suppress, merge-note, deprioritize, or otherwise mutate items without structural evidence and explicit approval batched by source/reason. Agenda duplicate/superseded/should-merge candidates approved for cleanup MUST complete with a note referencing the survivor/source. Bug Priority and feature Value remain user-owned and must not be requested until cleanup validation has completed for the candidate pool.

scenarios.title: Cleanup validation precedes issue creation and scoring prompts
given: /adv-triage has gathered sources and built represented[] plus unrepresented[] match results
then: Source cleanup validation has run before any new GH issue creation prompt
Source cleanup validation has run before any bug Priority prompt
Source cleanup validation has run before any feature Value prompt

scenarios.title: Cleanup candidates carry evidence and action shape
given: A source item, represented issue, active ADV change, or agenda item is stale, already addressed, duplicate, superseded, should-merge, or unclear
then: The report includes source and stable ref
The report includes classification, evidence, proposed action, and approval group
Unclear items are surfaced instead of silently suppressed

scenarios.title: Heuristic cleanup findings are advisory only
given: Title similarity, token overlap, or agent inference flags a cleanup candidate
scenarios.when: No structural evidence and no explicit user approval exists
then: /adv-triage does not close, complete, cancel, remove, suppress, merge-note, or deprioritize the item
The candidate remains surfaced for user confirmation or clarification

scenarios.title: Cleanup mutations require source-reason approval
given: A cleanup candidate would mutate or suppress an ADV change, GitHub issue/project item, agenda item, or local source
scenarios.when: /adv-triage prepares to apply the cleanup action
then: The action is included in a batch grouped by source/reason
Explicit approval is recorded before mutation or suppression
Bug Priority and feature Value prompts wait until the approval path resolves or the item is otherwise classified relevant

scenarios.title: Agenda superseded or merge candidates complete with survivor note
given: An agenda item is approved as duplicate/superseded or should-merge
then: The agenda item is completed rather than silently deleted
The completion note references the survivor/source

requirements.title: Planning and WIP Collision Detection for Linked Ops Work
requirements.body: Planning, WIP, and collision checks MUST surface active linked ops/enabler work from structural workflow state or Visibility-backed discovery, not from agenda text search. adv_wip_state and related planning readbacks MUST include active ops_followup_links and their status when queried in the context of a source/parent change. Collision detection MUST treat an active blocking ops_followup_link as an in-flight dependency.

scenarios.title: WIP state includes active ops follow-ups
given: A parent change has active ops_followup_links with status running
scenarios.when: adv_wip_state is queried for the project
then: The response includes an ops_follow_ups section
Each entry contains link ID, target, relationship, and status

scenarios.title: Planning collision uses structural state
given: /adv-prep evaluates a change whose parent has a blocks link that is not complete
then: The blocker is surfaced from ops_followup_links
No agenda text search is used as the correctness authority

scenarios.title: Cross-project ops link is discoverable
given: A parent in project A links to a child follow-up in project B
then: The query resolves the target through the product state plane
The status is surfaced in the WIP view
